---
title: "Teaching"
title-block-banner: false
body-class: teaching-page
---
<style>
/* Teaching page-local fixes */

#title-block-header .title,
.quarto-title h1.title {
  color: var(--navy) !important;
  font-family: "Libre Baskerville", Georgia, serif !important;
  font-size: clamp(4rem, 8vw, 5.5rem) !important;
  line-height: 0.95 !important;
  letter-spacing: -0.045em !important;
  margin-bottom: 2.25rem !important;
}

/* Keep section heading smaller than page title */
.teaching-page-wrap h2 {
  color: var(--navy) !important;
  font-family: "Libre Baskerville", Georgia, serif !important;
  font-size: clamp(2.1rem, 4vw, 2.7rem) !important;
  line-height: 1.15 !important;
  margin-top: 3rem !important;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem !important;
}

/* Give tag pills vertical breathing room */
.course-tags {
  display: flex !important;
  flex-wrap: wrap !important;
  column-gap: 0.55rem !important;
  row-gap: 1rem !important;
  margin-top: 1.75rem !important;
  align-items: flex-start !important;
}

.course-tags span {
  display: inline-flex !important;
  align-items: center !important;
  min-height: 2rem !important;
  line-height: 1.2 !important;
  margin: 0 0 0.35rem 0 !important;
  padding-top: 0.32rem !important;
  padding-bottom: 0.32rem !important;
}
</style>
::: {.teaching-page-wrap}


::: {.teaching-stats}

::: {.teaching-stat}
<span class="stat-number">10+</span>
<span class="stat-label">years of teaching</span>
:::

::: {.teaching-stat}
<span class="stat-number">3</span>
<span class="stat-label">recent course areas</span>
:::

::: {.teaching-stat}
<span class="stat-number">FR / EN</span>
<span class="stat-label">teaching contexts</span>
:::

:::

## Courses taught recently

::: {.teaching-course-list}

::: {.teaching-course-card}
<div class="course-code">ANG-1002</div>

### Introduction to Linguistics

<div class="course-meta">Université Laval · Fall 2025, Fall 2023, Fall 2022</div>

<div class="course-role">Instructor of record</div>

An introductory course covering the main areas of linguistic analysis, including sounds, words, sentences, meaning, language variation, and the structure of human language.

<div class="course-tags">
<span>phonetics</span>
<span>syntax</span>
<span>semantics</span>
<span>variation</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.teaching-course-card}
<div class="course-code">FRN-1110</div>

### Lexicology

<div class="course-meta">Université Laval · Winter 2025, Winter 2024</div>

<div class="course-role">Instructor of record</div>

A course on the study of the lexicon, with attention to word formation, lexical meaning, lexical relations, neology, dictionaries, and standard methods used to investigate these objects.

<div class="course-tags">
<span>lexicon</span>
<span>neology</span>
<span>dictionaries</span>
<span>word formation</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.teaching-course-card}
<div class="course-code">ANG-2502</div>

### Understanding Grammar

<div class="course-meta">Université Laval · Fall 2024</div>

<div class="course-role">Instructor of record</div>

A course focused on grammatical analysis and the tools used to describe language structure, with attention to how grammar works in actual language use.

[Course archive](https://web.archive.org/web/20240731222708/https://www.ulaval.ca/etudes/cours/ang-2502-understanding-grammar){.course-link}

<div class="course-tags">
<span>grammar</span>
<span>analysis</span>
<span>usage</span>
<span>structure</span>
</div>
:::

:::

:::
